name: rest-patterns
description: Quick reference for RESTful API design patterns, HTTP semantics, caching, and rate limiting. Triggers on: rest api, http methods, status codes, api design, endpoint design, api versioning, rate limiting, caching headers.
capabilities: Lookup HTTP methods and when to use each (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)
Reference HTTP status codes with usage examples for 2xx, 4xx, and 5xx responses
Apply caching strategies using Cache-Control, ETag, and conditional requests
Implement rate limiting with token bucket, sliding window, and leaky bucket patterns
Structure error responses following RFC 7807 and JSON:API formats
Design pagination, filtering, and HATEOAS links for REST collections
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown reference material about REST API patterns. No code execution, network calls, file system access beyond its own files, or command execution capabilities. All 204 static findings are false positives from the scanner misinterpreting markdown code formatting.
valueStatement: Designing RESTful APIs correctly requires understanding HTTP methods, status codes, caching strategies, and rate limiting. This skill provides quick reference patterns so you can build standards-compliant APIs from the start.
limitations: Does not generate actual API code or implementations
Does not validate existing API designs against standards
Does not test or monitor live API endpoints
Does not replace API documentation tools or swagger specs

useCases.title: Design new API endpoints
useCases.description: Choose correct HTTP methods, status codes, and response formats when designing new REST endpoints.

useCases.title: Implement API caching
useCases.description: Configure Cache-Control headers, ETags, and conditional requests to optimize API performance.

useCases.title: Review API designs
useCases.description: Audit existing APIs for proper error handling, rate limiting, and REST compliance.

promptTemplates.title: Basic status codes
promptTemplates.prompt: What status code should I return when a user creates a new resource successfully?

promptTemplates.title: Caching strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure caching for user-specific data that should never be stored in shared caches?

promptTemplates.title: Rate limiting
promptTemplates.prompt: What headers should I include in responses to inform clients about their rate limits?

promptTemplates.title: Error handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the RFC 7807 format for error responses and give examples for validation errors.

bestPractices: Use the correct HTTP method: GET for reads, POST for creates, PUT for replacements, PATCH for partial updates, DELETE for removals.
Return 201 with Location header for successful POST requests so clients know where the new resource exists.
Configure Cache-Control with no-store for sensitive data and use ETags with If-Match for optimistic locking on updates.
antiPatterns: Using verbs in URLs like /getUsers instead of /users with GET method.
Returning 200 OK for all responses including errors instead of proper 4xx/5xx status codes.
Returning 404 for authorization failures (use 403 instead) or vice versa (401 for unauthenticated, 403 for unauthorized).

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Trigger phrases include REST API, HTTP methods, status codes, and API design.

faq.question: What are the main HTTP methods?
faq.answer: GET retrieves data, POST creates resources, PUT replaces entirely, PATCH updates partially, and DELETE removes resources.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination?
faq.answer: Use query parameters like page and limit. Return total count and total pages in the response metadata for client navigation.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only contains documentation. It does not access files, network, or execute code on your system.

faq.question: Why is my API slow?
faq.answer: Check caching headers. Use ETags for conditional requests and Cache-Control with appropriate max-age values for different resource types.

faq.question: How is this different from Swagger?
faq.answer: This skill provides quick reference patterns. Swagger generates actual documentation and client libraries from API definitions.
